quote: Jewels five-words-long, That on the stretch'd forefinger of all Time Sparkle forever.

quote: Ring out the old, ring in the new, Ring, happy bells, across the snow!

author: Alfred Tennyson
quote: We are ancients of the earth, And in the morning of the times.

author: Henry W. Longfellow

quote: Our torments also may in length of time Become our elements.

quote: The childhood shows the man, As morning shows the day.

quote: Till morning fair Came forth with pilgrim steps in amice gray.

quote: Till old experience do attain To something like prophetic strain.

quote: Was I deceiv'd, or did a sable cloud Turn forth her silver lining on the night?

author: Robert Browning

author: Robert Burns
quote: Stern Ruin's ploughshare drives elate Full on thy bloom.

source_collection: John Bartlett, Familiar Quotations, 9th ed. (1905), Project Gutenberg eBook #27889

author: Edgar A. Poe

source_collection: John Bartlett, Familiar Quotations, 9th ed. (1905), Project Gutenberg eBook #27889

source_collection: John Bartlett, Familiar Quotations, 9th ed. (1905), Project Gutenberg eBook #27889

quote: Who can tell Men's hearts? The purest comprehend Such contradictions, and can blend The force to bear, the power to feel, The tender bud, the tempered steel.

quote: He who seeks wealth sacrifices his own pleasure, and, like him who carries burdens for others, bears the load of anxiety.
source_collection: W. A. Clouston, Book of Wise Sayings (1893), Project Gutenberg eBook #21130

source_collection: John Bartlett, Familiar Quotations, 9th ed. (1905), Project Gutenberg eBook #27889

source_collection: John Bartlett, Familiar Quotations, 9th ed. (1905), Project Gutenberg eBook #27889

quote: Gashed with honourable scars, Low in Glory's lap they lie; Though they fell, they fell like stars, Streaming splendour through the sky.
source_collection: John Bartlett, Familiar Quotations, 9th ed. (1905), Project Gutenberg eBook #27889

author: James Russell Lowell
quote: Dear common flower, that grow'st beside the way, Fringing the dusty road with harmless gold.
source_collection: John Bartlett, Familiar Quotations, 9th ed. (1905), Project Gutenberg eBook #27889

author: James Russell Lowell
quote: Ez to my princerples, I glory In hevin' nothin' o' the sort.

author: John Ferriar
quote: Now cheaply bought for thrice their weight in gold.

author: John Ferriar
quote: The princeps copy, clad in blue and gold.

author: Jonathan Swift
quote: The two noblest things, which are sweetness and light.
source_collection: John Bartlett, Familiar Quotations, 9th ed. (1905), Project Gutenberg eBook #27889

author: Joseph Addison
quote: And those that paint them truest praise them most.
source_collection: John Bartlett, Familiar Quotations, 9th ed. (1905), Project Gutenberg eBook #27889

quote: When vice prevails, and impious men bear sway, The post of honour is a private station.

author: Joseph Hopkinson
quote: The imperfect offices of prayer and praise.
source_collection: John Bartlett, Familiar Quotations, 9th ed. (1905), Project Gutenberg eBook #27889

author: Joseph Hopkinson
quote: The sunshine is a glorious birth; But yet I know, where'er I go, That there hath passed away a glory from the earth.

author: Joseph Hopkinson
quote: Turning, for them who pass, the common dust Of servile opportunity to gold.
source_collection: John Bartlett, Familiar Quotations, 9th ed. (1905), Project Gutenberg eBook #27889

quote: The Assyrian came down like the wolf on the fold, And his cohorts were gleaming in purple and gold.

source_collection: John Bartlett, Familiar Quotations, 9th ed. (1905), Project Gutenberg eBook #27889

author: Oliver Goldsmith
quote: His best companions, innocence and health; And his best riches, ignorance of wealth.

author: Oliver Goldsmith
quote: Luke's iron crown, and Damien's bed of steel.

quote: It is better to be well deserving without praise than to live by the air of undeserved commendation.

source_collection: John Bartlett, Familiar Quotations, 9th ed. (1905), Project Gutenberg eBook #27889

quote: The alleged power to charm down insanity, or ferocity in beasts, is a power behind the eye.

source_collection: John Bartlett, Familiar Quotations, 9th ed. (1905), Project Gutenberg eBook #27889

author: Richter
quote: When anyone is modest, not after praise, but after censure, then he is really so.
source_collection: W. A. Clouston, Book of Wise Sayings (1893), Project Gutenberg eBook #21130

author: Robert Browning

quote: No one ought to despond in adverse circumstances, for they may turn out to be the cause of good to us.
